name: DuckBot Brain
description: Local hybrid RAG with cross-encoder rerank (Layer 7), Ebbinghaus decay (Layer 8), and verbatim-first storage (Layer 13). Connects the DuckBot brain project (`~/Desktop/duckbot-rag-memory`) to OpenClaw as a memory provider. MIT, zero paid APIs, runs entirely on the local machine.

pythonPath.description: Path to the python interpreter that can import duckbot-rag-memory. Defaults to the venv at <repo>/.venv/bin/python.

repoPath.description: Absolute path to the duckbot-rag-memory checkout. Defaults to ~/Desktop/duckbot-rag-memory.

tools.name: brain_wake_up
tools.description: ONE-CALL session-startup context load. Returns recent memories (superseded filtered), active memory blocks, graph summary, FSRS review queue, and brief stats. Call this first on every session start. MemPalace-inspired. With query='', pulls most-recent episodic + procedural chunks.

tools.name: brain_recall
tools.description: Hybrid retrieval (vector + BM25 + RRF). Optionally rerank=true for cross-encoder boost, decay=true for Ebbinghaus retention weighting.

tools.name: brain_recall_verbatim
tools.description: Returns the original (pre-overlap, pre-prefix) source text. Use for 'what exactly did I say?' queries.

tools.name: brain_remember
tools.description: Persist text to the brain (async, non-blocking; rate-limited 10/min). Pass kind='skill_candidate' to stamp a lightweight skill candidate (agent-driven pipeline, no LLM, stored in procedural tier for later promotion).

kind.description: special mode: stamp a skill candidate chunk (no LLM)

tools.name: brain_reflect
tools.description: Sleep-time consolidation: merge episodic chunks into the semantic tier. Long-running; call once per cron / day.

tools.name: brain_stats
tools.description: Return brain stats: chunk counts per tier, graph entities, block count, quarantine totals, last query time.

tools.name: brain_fsrs_review
tools.description: Return chunks due for FSRS-6 spaced-repetition review (R(t,S) < 0.9). Public-domain math, no LLM.

tools.name: brain_decay_status
tools.description: Return Ebbinghaus decay status (R = e^(-t/S)) for recent chunks, grouped by tier. Public-domain math (1885), no LLM.

tools.name: brain_search_verbatim
tools.description: Exact substring match against the verbatim (pre-overlap) text. Useful when you remember a phrase verbatim.

tools.name: brain_skills_list
tools.description: List unpromoted skill-candidate chunks (agent-driven pipeline). The agent reads these and decides which to promote. No LLM call.

tools.name: brain_skills_suggest
tools.description: Semantic top-N skill candidates matching a query (agent-driven pipeline). Uses hybrid retrieval scoped to the procedural tier and filtered to unpromoted candidates. Use when the agent is working on a topic and wants to know 'are there candidate skills about X?'. No LLM call.

tools.name: brain_skills_promote
tools.description: Promote a skill candidate to a full SKILL.md. The AGENT authors name/description/instructions — the brain is pure storage + template (no LLM). Writes skills/<slug>/SKILL.md and marks the candidate promoted.
